data.title: Omnicell upgraded at Wells Fargo on positive risk-reward setup
data.description:
Wells Fargo upgraded Omnicell (NASDAQ:OMCL) to Overweight from Equal Weight on Wednesday, citing a compelling risk-reward setup in the shares of the Fort Worth, Texas-based vendor of pharmacy automation solutions.

Analyst Stan Berenshteyn also raised his price target on OMCL to $35 from $31 per share, noting that the ongoing U.S.-China trade talks are likely to yield positive results for the company, which has lost more than a third of its value YTD amid the Trump administration’s newly imposed tariffs.

“While OMCL is still likely to pursue supply chain channel shifts outside of China to geographies with higher costs vs. pre-liberation day, we see upside to the current tariff regime as likely,” Berenshteyn wrote, referring to the tariffs President Trump announced in early April.

Additionally, citing a survey of health systems, the analyst argued that, despite near-term pressure from an end to XT upgrades, the company’s bookings growth will likely accelerate in 2026 thanks to upgrades and robot adoption.

“While 2025 bookings may remain challenged, we see prospects for improving demand in 2026+ to balance near-term risks,” Berenshteyn wrote.

data.title: California dreaming: These companies have ties to tribal casinos in the Golden State
data.description:
While casino gambling in California is limited to tribal casinos, several publicly traded companies are deepening their involvement in the industry, a sector traditionally dominated by federally recognized tribes. While 

State and federal laws in California require that tribal casinos remain under tribal ownership and control. However, recently, there has been a rise in creative partnerships, financing arrangements, and management agreements with major corporations.

One of the most significant casino developments in California is the recent agreement between Gaming and Leisure Properties, Inc. (NASDAQ:GLPI) and a California tribe to finance and lease the upcoming Acorn Ridge Casino in Amador County. Gaming and Leisure Properties (NASDAQ:GLPI) has committed $110 million in loans and secured an option for a long-term lease arrangement, marking the first such deal in the state. The management team has described this as a potential model for future collaborations, offering tribes access to capital and operational expertise while maintaining tribal ownership and regulatory compliance.

Meanwhile, Hard Rock International plays a significant and growing role in California’s casino industry through high-profile partnerships with Native American tribes, acting as both a developer and operator of major casino-resort projects. The company’s most prominent presence is at the Hard Rock Hotel & Casino Sacramento at Fire Mountain, which operates in partnership with the Enterprise Rancheria of Estom Yumeka Maidu. Hard Rock manages the property under a formal agreement, bringing its global brand, operational expertise, and entertainment focus to the Northern California market. Hard Rock International is also developing a major new $600 million project in partnership with the Tejon Indian Tribe in Kern County. The Hard Rock Hotel & Casino Tejon is scheduled to open its casino by December 2025

Red Rock Resorts (NASDAQ:RRR), through its operational arm Station Casinos, is actively moving forward with a significant project in California. The company has secured a management and development agreement with the North Fork Rancheria of Mono Indians to build and operate the North Fork Project, a major casino and entertainment facility in Madera County, just north of Fresno. This project, which has faced legal challenges for over two decades, is now advancing after receiving federal approval for its management agreement. Upon completion, the North Fork Project will feature a 100,000-square-foot casino with over 2,000 slot and video poker machines, 40 table games, dining options, and ample parking.

VICI Properties (NYSE:VICI) recently announced its commitment for up to $510 million as part of a $725 million delayed draw term loan facility to fund the development of the North Fork Mono Casino & Resort in Madera.

Analysts have suggested that the companies with an early presence in California could have a distinct advantage if broad casino gambling is approved down the road.

data.title: European Wax Center reiterates $69M–$71M adjusted EBITDA outlook while targeting return to net unit growth by end of 2026
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: European Wax Center (EWCZ) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Chris Morris, Chairman and CEO, reported solid first quarter performance with $225.9 million in system-wide sales, 70 basis points of positive same-store sales growth, and $18.8 million in adjusted EBITDA. Morris emphasized, “These results demonstrate that our guests value our unparalleled waxing services and our business is on the right track, enabling us to reiterate our outlook today.”

  • Morris discussed his first 100 days, stating his belief in EWC’s potential is stronger than ever. He highlighted the year 2025 as a “reset year,” focusing on “modernizing the marketing engine, making sure franchisees are set up for success and being disciplined and strategic in our approach to new center expansion.”

  • A sharpened vision centers on “driving sales, improving 4-wall profitability and reigniting unit growth,” with early signs of traction in new guest trends and marketing efficiency. Morris also announced the addition of new executive leaders including Chief Commercial Officer Katie Mullen, Chief Information and Digital Officer Chris Andrews, and Chief Financial Officer Tom Kim.

  • Tom Kim, CFO, stated, “EWC's category leadership position, meaningful white space and strong free cash flow profile offer a compelling and attractive opportunity.” Kim reported ending Q1 with 1,062 centers, $51.4 million in total revenue, and 36.5% adjusted EBITDA margin. He noted, “Q1 revenue exceeded our internal expectations due to franchisee order patterns and a successful retail promotion, both of which we believe pulled forward some demand.”

Outlook

  • The company reiterated its full-year 2025 guidance, expecting system-wide sales between $940 million and $960 million and same-store sales flat to up 2%. Full-year revenue is projected between $210 million and $214 million, with adjusted EBITDA in the range of $69 million to $71 million. Adjusted net income is expected between $31 million and $33 million. Kim stated, “Our outlook assumptions for the full year remain intact.”

  • The company expects 10 to 12 gross openings and 40 to 60 center closures, or 28 to 50 net center closures in 2025, with a return to net unit growth targeted by the end of 2026.

  • Management highlighted that the high end of guidance assumes marketing efforts will drive more traffic in the back half of 2025, while the low end assumes continued modest transaction declines in mature centers.

Financial Results

  • System-wide sales increased 2.1% year-over-year to $225.9 million, and same-store sales grew 70 basis points. Total revenue was $51.4 million, down approximately $400,000, mainly due to lower product revenue. Adjusted EBITDA rose to $18.8 million, and adjusted net income increased 10.3% to $9.5 million. Gross margin improved modestly to 74.2%.

  • Advertising expense decreased by $1.4 million due to timing, and SG&A increased by $1.9 million, driven by higher stock-based compensation and executive severance costs. The company ended the quarter with $58.3 million in cash and $389 million in outstanding senior secured notes, and net leverage ratio was 4.3x.

Q&A

  • Dana Telsey, Telsey Advisory Group, asked about Wax Pass customer trends and tariffs. Morris responded the business is “stabilizing and we’re starting to see some early tractions on all of our strategies,” and that tariff risks are being managed with all options considered but no change expected to the core wax formula.

  • Jonathan Komp, Baird, inquired about increased build costs and unit growth. Morris explained the rise is “just simply inflationary pressure that everybody has been feeling,” and the return to net unit growth will focus on markets with sufficient density and franchisee ROI.

  • John Heinbockel, Guggenheim, questioned underperforming centers. Morris said closures are “mainly an AUV issue,” with underperformance due to top line pressure, and highlighted efforts in digital marketing and brand identity refinement to drive new guest acquisition.

  • Korinne Wolfmeyer, Piper Sandler, asked about consumer trends and advertising spend. Morris described the core guest as “resilient,” and Kim confirmed a more even marketing spend through the year.

  • Kelly Crago, Citi, asked about underperforming stores and sales transfer. Morris noted about 75% of sales come from Wax Pass holders and stated, “we have no immediate plans to change” that mix.

  • Simeon Gutman, Morgan Stanley, inquired about priorities between marketing, franchisee execution, waxer consistency, and value to consumers. Morris confirmed marketing and execution as top priorities, with waxer consistency and consumer value following.

  • Scot Ciccarelli, Truist, requested specifics on marketing changes and tariffs. Morris detailed improvements in paid media efficiency and creative testing, while reiterating confidence in managing tariff impacts within guidance.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ questions centered on operational challenges, underperforming units, and marketing strategy, with a neutral tone and interest in incremental progress rather than skepticism.

  • Management maintained a positive and confident tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, using phrases like “we feel great,” “we are very pleased,” and “we feel very confident.”

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts maintained consistent sentiment, focused on stabilization and incremental improvement, with no notable increase in negativity or defensiveness.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language is unchanged, with the company reiterating system-wide sales, revenue, and adjusted EBITDA targets.

  • Strategic focus continues on marketing modernization, franchisee support, and disciplined unit expansion.

  • Key metrics, including system-wide sales growth and same-store sales, showed marginal improvement, while the pace of closures and new openings aligns with the previous quarter’s outlook.

  • Management’s tone remained positive and confident, with ongoing emphasis on foundational improvements and long-term growth.

  • Analyst focus remained on closures, marketing effectiveness, consumer trends, and cost pressures, mirroring the previous quarter’s inquiries.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited an uncertain consumer and supply chain environment, with tariff exposure on certain product components. Morris stated, “our team is maniacally focused on looking at all ways of managing through tariff exposure.”

  • The company is addressing underperforming centers, mainly driven by AUV shortfalls, and franchisee profitability pressures from past rapid growth and inflation.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the impact of closures, the ability to drive new guest acquisition, and the execution of marketing initiatives.

Final Takeaway

European Wax Center management reaffirmed confidence in the business model and long-term growth potential, emphasizing early success in marketing and operational initiatives, stable core guest behavior, and a clear path to reignite unit growth by the end of 2026. The company continues to prioritize driving traffic, improving franchisee profitability, and managing risks such as tariffs and inflation within current guidance, while maintaining a strong financial position and disciplined focus on execution.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Quick highlights from Disney's Upfront presentation this year
data.description:
Media conglomerate Disney (NYSE:DIS) put on a star-studded show at its 2025 Upfront presentation in New York on Tuesday. The event showcased company executives, sporting legends, and celebrities who shared details about upcoming projects. Here is a quick recap: 

Sports

  • The company spoke about the upcoming Monday Night Football season and Super Bowl LXI coming to ABC and ESPN in 2027.
  • Disney said it will be "the exclusive home of the NBA Finals," with ten exclusive NBA Conference Finals through 2035, along with five NBA games on Christmas Day.
  • A brand-new female-led sports show, Vibe Check, will debut on ESPN that will highlight "the biggest stories" in sports.
  • The Kingdom, an upcoming six-episode ESPN Original Series about the history of the Kansas City Chiefs, was announced.
  • Chad Powers, an upcoming Hulu comedy series about college football, is set to premiere on September 30.

Television

  • The company said ABC News content will now be available on Disney+.
  • The company unveiled an early look for Alien: Earth, based on the Alien franchise, which will premiere on August 12.
  • Season four of Emmy Award-winning series The Bear to be available on Hulu on June 25.
  • A first look for FX’s upcoming series The Beauty, starring Evan Peters, Anthony Ramos, Jeremy Pope, and Ashton Kutcher, was shown.
  • Teaser trailer shown for new show All's Fair, starring Kim Kardashian, Glenn Close, Sarah Paulson, Niecy Nash-Betts, and Teyana Taylor.
  • The company teased the theatrical release of Zootopia 2.
  • Krysten Ritter would reprise her role as Jessica Jones and join Charlie Cox in season two of Daredevil: Born Again.
  • Ironheart, a Ryan Coogler produced series, to release on June 24.
  • Wonder Man, a meta-Hollywood 8 episode series coming in December, which will star Yahya Abdul-Mateen II.

data.title: Airbnb's expanded offerings may boost revenue, but analysts hold their ratings
data.description:
After announcing an expansion of its services available to both travelers and non-travelers, analysts weighed in on Airbnb’s (NASDAQ:ABNB) Experiences and Services offerings, and what this means for the company’s top-line.

Airbnb (NASDAQ:ABNB) is attempting to rival traditional hotel offerings with a menu of options that include spa treatments, tour guides, personal chefs, and even a one-on-one with a professional athlete, all within the platform that also allows Airbnb (NASDAQ:ABNB) to provide travel updates and recommendations and for travel companions to communicate with one another within the app.

Evercore ISI analyst Mark Mahaney came away from the announcement “incrementally more positive on Airbnb’s ‘beyond the core’ initiative,” and sees a potential for these offerings to eventually scale and help reaccelerate bookings, revenue, and profits.

“Together these new products aim to turn Airbnb into a full-scale ‘lifestyle platform,’” Mahaney said.

By offering Services and Experiences while on vacation or at home, this potentially expands Airbnb’s (ABNB) opportunities while at the same time provides a more “always on use” case during trips, BofA Securities’ Justin Post and Nafeesa Gupta said.

The BofA team did the math, and came to some compelling outcomes that could eventually juice up the company’s bottom-line. They assume a 15% booking rate by guests for Services priced at $150. This yields a “potential 3.5% incremental revenue uplift opportunity.”

For Experiences, to which Airbnb (ABNB) has a more “captive” audience, the team assumes a modest 10% of guests on trips will book a $250 experience for their group. This leads to a potential 5.3% incremental revenue lift opportunity (which could be higher if non-guests also book).

“So assuming a relatively modest guest adoption of Services (15%) and Experiences (10%), we estimate a potential 8% core revenue uplift opportunity by 2030, translating into ~$1.5B.”

While this is good news for the company’s top-line, it might not translate into stock gains in the near-term.

Both BofA Securities and Evercore ISI maintain their Neutral/In-Line rating on the stock despite the potential lift to revenue, with Mahaney saying he’s “waiting for evidence that ABNB can reverse its multi-year deceleration trajectory and reaccelerate revenue and profit growth.”

“Increase the amenities accessible via Airbnb and you might actually increase the bookings. Seems like a reasonable thesis to us,” he adds.

Airbnb (ABNB) shares remain in the green for a fourth consecutive week for the first time in more than a year and are up 6% YTD.

data.title: Allurion outlines path to FDA submission and expects revenue ramp as B2B2C strategy expands in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Allurion Technologies Inc. (ALUR) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Shantanu Gaur, CEO, stated the company "delivered the first quarter in line with expectations, setting us up to deliver on the year ahead." He highlighted $5.6 million in revenue and a 45% decrease in adjusted operating expenses. Gaur emphasized progress on five pillars: a new commercial plan with deeper penetration in key geographies using a B2B2C direct sales model, pursuit of FDA approval for the Allurion Balloon, a goal of achieving ex-U.S. profitability by year-end, scalability of the AI product platform, and resumption of commercialization in France.
  • Gaur reported, "We piloted several aspects of the B2B2C model in clinics in Europe in the first quarter and observed over 40% growth quarter-over-quarter and year-over-year." He noted new sales hires and expects revenues to ramp as the year progresses.
  • The CEO discussed new clinical data, including two studies presented at the European Congress on Obesity, underscoring the versatility of Allurion's product portfolio and the potential for new revenue streams from combining their program with GLP-1 therapies.
  • Gaur also detailed the completion of a pre-PMA meeting with the FDA and expects to complete the PMA submission by the end of June. He reported cash and cash equivalents of $20 million at quarter-end, which provides "the runway we believe we need to achieve our FDA approval, profitability, and other milestones that could generate significant value for shareholders."
  • Tara Brady, Interim CFO, stated, "Our revenue for the first quarter of 2025 was $5.6 million compared to $9.4 million for the same period in 2024. The year-over-year decrease in revenue was primarily due to the temporary suspension of sales in France as well as lower investments in sales and marketing." Brady highlighted a gross margin of 75% and the impact of restructuring on reducing expenses and narrowing losses.

Outlook

  • Gaur confirmed, "We are maintaining our guidance of revenues of approximately $3 million with a reduction in operating expenses of approximately 50% compared to 2024." He expects revenues to ramp through 2025 as the B2B2C model expands and new sales hires are onboarded.
  • Management anticipates no impact on gross margin from tariffs and expects further updates on the France relaunch later in the year.
  • Gaur shared plans for a prospective clinical trial combining the Allurion Balloon with low-dose GLP-1s, targeting enrollment by year-end 2025 and aiming to establish a new standard-of-care in obesity treatment.

Financial Results

  • The company reported $5.6 million in revenue for Q1 2025. Gross profit was $4.2 million, or 75% of revenue.
  • Sales and marketing expenses were $3.6 million. Research and development expenses were $2.6 million, and general and administrative expenses were $5.2 million, with $3.8 million adjusted after excluding one-time financing costs.
  • Adjusted loss from operations was $5.9 million, reflecting a narrowing loss due to restructuring and gross margin expansion. Cash and cash equivalents stood at $20.4 million at quarter-end.

Q&A

  • Jason Wittes, ROTH Capital Partners, asked about regional trends and the impact of the B2B2C strategy. Gaur responded that mature GLP-1 markets are generating tailwinds, and as the sales force grows, "we expect revenues to steadily ramp as the year goes on."
  • Wittes inquired about the GLP-1 trial design and timeline. Gaur indicated enrollment would begin in the latter half of 2025, with at least 75 subjects, and results expected after a one-year follow-up.
  • Wittes asked about gross margin sustainability. Gaur said, "As the year goes on, we expect margins to remain in that ballpark...I would say what we did in the first quarter, we should be in that same ballpark for the remainder of the year."
  • Josh Jennings, TD Cowen, asked for specifics on the FDA path and control group analyses. Gaur explained, "We were very pleased with FDA's openness to considering additional analyses...we will have, I believe, a much more accurate representation of that control group performance."
  • Jennings inquired about GLP-1 usage in control arms and commercial adoption of combination therapy. Gaur noted combination therapy is happening organically and that registry data via the Virtual Care Suite will help track global performance.
  • Jennings queried about sequential revenue build and France's recovery. Gaur expects France to contribute to placements in the second half of 2025, with material revenue impact in 2026.
  • Keay Nakae, Chardan, asked about the cost and regulatory intent of the combination trial. Gaur stated the trial would not materially impact the budget and is designed more for commercial and clinical validation than immediate regulatory label expansion.
  • Mike Toomey, Jefferies, questioned procedure growth and tariff impacts. Gaur responded, "Procedures and our top line revenue are moving in lockstep...we see stability in procedure volume ex-France."
  • Toomey asked about Virtual Care Suite revenue. Gaur explained material contributions are expected post-U.S. ramp, not in 2025 or early 2026.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on the specifics of the new commercial strategy, trial design, margin sustainability, and the FDA process, with a neutral-to-positive tone as they sought clarity on execution and timing.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone throughout both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently highlighting operational progress, margin expansion, and regulatory milestones. Gaur used phrases like "we are excited" and "we believe" to signal confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained confident but with more details provided on execution and specific clinical and regulatory initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management reiterated the same five-pillar strategy as the prior quarter but provided updates on execution, particularly B2B2C pilot results and FDA progress.
  • Gross margin rebounded from 45% in Q4 2024 to 75% in Q1 2025, attributed to manufacturing efficiencies and restructuring.
  • Revenue remained stable quarter-over-quarter but declined year-over-year due to the suspension in France.
  • The tone from analysts remained constructive, with continued focus on commercialization cadence, regional trends, and margin recovery, while management maintained strong confidence and offered more granular updates.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified the temporary suspension of sales in France and lower investments in sales and marketing as primary reasons for the year-over-year revenue decrease.
  • Gaur stated direct exposure to tariffs is negligible and does not expect any impact on gross margin from tariffs for the remainder of the year.
  • The company expects the cost of new clinical trials to be manageable within its existing budget and does not anticipate significant additional expense.

Final Takeaway

Allurion entered 2025 with a streamlined business model, improved operating efficiency, and a clear plan to expand the B2B2C strategy and pursue FDA approval for the Allurion Balloon. Management expects revenue growth to accelerate throughout the year as new sales hires come online and the commercial strategy matures, while maintaining a disciplined approach to costs and capital allocation. The company sees the combination of its program with GLP-1 therapies as a catalyst for future growth and is positioning itself for sustainable profitability and potential regulatory milestones in the coming quarters.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Microsoft likely to avoid large antitrust fine from EU after Office-Teams offer: report
data.description:
• Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) is likely to avoid being penalized a hefty amount from the European Union after its latest offer on its Office and Teams products, Reuters reported.
  • Some proposals from the Windows maker include better interoperability terms and make it easier for the competition, the news outlet added, citing three people familiar with the matter.
  • The European Commission is still likely to talk to rivals and customers over the next few months before a final decision is made, the people added.
  • Microsoft came under fire when Slack, owned by Salesforce (CRM), complained in 2020 to the E.C. that Microsoft received an unfair advantage by bundling Office and Teams together.
  • Microsoft and the European Commission did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: AbbVie wins approval of Emrelis for non-small cell lung cancer
data.description:
• The U.S. FDA on Wednesday granted accelerated approval to AbbVie's (NYSE:ABBV) Emrelis (telisotuzumab vedotin), an antibody-drug conjugate that is a second-line therapy for non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer with high c-Met protein overexpression.
  • Approval was based on results from a phase 2 trial that showed patients treated with Emrelis showed improvements in overall response rate and duration of response.
  • AbbVie said that Emrelis is the first treatment approved for previously treated advanced NSCLC patients with high c-Met protein overexpression.
  • The FDA also approved Roche's (OTCQX:RHHBY) VENTANA MET (SP44) RxDx Assay, a companion diagnostic that identifies patients suited for treatment with Emrelis.

data.title: Nyxoah outlines U.S. Genio launch plan with 50-member team as FDA approval expected Q2 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Nyxoah (NYXH) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Olivier Taelman emphasized the pivotal progress toward full PMA approval in the United States, confirming, "the FDA issued an approvable letter for our Genio system which confirms that our application substantially meets the requirements for marketing in the U.S." He reported the successful completion and FDA acceptance of required manufacturing validation, stating, "FDA reviewed this validation already and has confirmed they have no further questions regarding it." The final step is an on-site inspection at the U.S. contract manufacturing facility, which is ongoing and previously passed without deficiencies. Taelman expressed confidence in receiving PMA approval in Q2 2025 and outlined the U.S. launch strategy: "We are ready to launch in the U.S. with 50 commercial team members who are all hired and trained." The strategy focuses on targeting high-volume hypoglossal nerve stimulation (HGNS) centers and developing networks with sleep physicians managing moderate to severe obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) populations.

  • Taelman described the approach as a "two-pronged" strategy with a "smart follower" model, leveraging demand for alternatives to pacemaker-based technologies and differentiating on Genio’s unique features, including bilateral stimulation and a battery-free device. The company highlighted the readiness of U.S. teams in sales, marketing, reimbursement, and medical affairs, along with ongoing efforts for payer education and the use of CPT code 64568 for reimbursement.

  • CFO John Landry stated, "We recorded revenue of €1.1 million in the first quarter of 2025 compared to €1.2 million in the first quarter of 2024... The slight year-over-year revenue decrease was primarily due to temporary softness in the international HGNS market." He noted a total operating loss of €20.6 million, attributed to U.S. commercial investments, and reported a cash position of €63 million at March 31, 2025.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated confidence in receiving FDA PMA approval in the second quarter of 2025. Taelman stated, "we continue to expect to receive PMA approval in the second quarter of 2025."

  • The company plans to scale the U.S. commercial team each quarter post-approval and expects pricing to be aligned with competitors, supported by Genio’s technology differentiation. Landry indicated that full-year R&D spending is projected to be in line with 2024, while sales and marketing expenses are expected to be "a little bit more than double what we had incurred in 2024."

Financial Results

  • Landry reported revenue of €1.1 million for Q1 2025 and an operating loss of €20.6 million, with a cash position of €63 million at quarter-end. The revenue dip was linked to temporary international market softness and a phased rollout of the Genio 2.1 software upgrade, which led some sites to defer purchases until its availability. Landry explained, "We expect these factors will normalise in the upcoming quarters."

Q&A

  • Jon Block, Stifel, asked about the ongoing FDA inspection. Taelman explained, "the site inspection is actively taking place as we speak... After this, he will finish his report... And that's also why we stay confident that we will obtain the approval by the end of the quarter."

  • Adam Maeder, Piper Sandler, inquired about statutory response times for FDA approval and the DREAM Journal publication. Taelman clarified, "there is currently an inspection that is taking place... We are feeling confident that we will obtain the FDA approval." He also indicated that DREAM publication is in review and expected before summer.

  • Maeder asked about OpEx pace. Landry responded, "we've taken steps to defer costs and the investments until our FDA approval is achieved... extend our cash runway as well into the second quarter of 2026."

  • Ross Osborn, Cantor Fitzgerald, queried about Genio’s Dubai launch and ACCCESS study. Taelman described milestone implants in Dubai and ongoing expansion in the Middle East; for ACCCESS, "we keep making great progress in enrolling patients... stopping the enrollment by summer."

  • Suraj Kalia, Oppenheimer, asked about inventory management and DTC approach. Taelman stated inventory from competitors would not hinder Genio’s rollout and outlined a focused, non-mass-market DTC strategy.

  • David Rescott, Baird, asked about Genio 2.1 rollout and reimbursement execution. Taelman and Landry described the patient-centric software and readiness for U.S. launch with the established CPT code.

  • Michael Polark, Wolfe Research, sought clarity on CCC labeling. Taelman clarified that CCC would not be a contraindication, but also not yet on-label.

  • Laura Roba, Degroof Petercam, requested more on cash runway levers and market softness. Landry cited deferred discretionary spending and expects international market softness to be temporary.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were focused on clarity around FDA approval timing, commercial strategy, cash runway, and product differentiation, with a tone that was neutral to slightly positive, seeking reassurance on regulatory and operational execution.

  • Management maintained a consistently confident and measured tone, particularly in responses about FDA interactions and U.S. launch readiness, often reiterating timelines and expressing certainty: "we are feeling confident that we will obtain the FDA approval."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment remained steady with management sustaining high confidence, and analysts maintaining a wait-and-see approach, particularly on regulatory timing and commercial execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • FDA approval timing shifted from an expectation by end of March 2025 in Q4 to Q2 2025 in Q1, reflecting the ongoing site inspection. The commercial launch plan remains consistent, with the U.S. team fully prepared and 50 commercial hires in place both quarters. The company continued to highlight the same reimbursement code and two-pronged market entry strategy. Financially, Q1 revenue saw a slight decline from Q1 2024, attributed to temporary international market softness and delayed purchases pending the Genio 2.1 software upgrade. Operating loss increased quarter-over-quarter, driven by U.S. launch investments. Analyst focus remained on regulatory progress, cash runway, and commercial preparedness, while management confidence in the U.S. opportunity and regulatory outcome persisted.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged temporary softness in the international HGNS market and attributed the Q1 revenue dip to this and the Genio 2.1 phased rollout. They expect normalization in coming quarters. Landry stated, "We also saw our competitor in the first quarter also posted a mid-single-digit decline in the international market. We saw some softness there, seasonality. We expect it to be temporary." Discretionary spending has been deferred to extend the cash runway. The remaining risk centers on the pending FDA on-site inspection and subsequent approval timeline.

Final Takeaway

Nyxoah's management reiterated strong confidence in securing FDA approval for Genio in Q2 2025, with all U.S. commercial infrastructure in place for an immediate launch. Temporary revenue softness in international markets is expected to recover, and significant resources have been allocated to ensure a successful U.S. entry. The company is positioning Genio as a differentiated, patient-centric solution with robust clinical data, supported by established reimbursement pathways and a highly trained team, aiming to drive substantial growth after regulatory clearance.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SA Asks: Can UnitedHealth turn it around?
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group's (NYSE:UNH) stock has plunged nearly 40% year-to-date as the healthcare company has grappled with crisis after crisis, including the aftermath of a massive cyberattack, the murder of a top executive, unexpectedly higher costs, and federal investigations into its business practices.

Shares of the managed care giant were battered yet again on Tuesday after the company withdrew its 2025 financial guidance and announced that CEO Andrew Witty had resigned. Witty will be succeeded by UnitedHealth Chairman Stephen Hemsley, who served as also CEO of the company from 2006 to 2017.

So, what's next for UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH)? Can the healthcare juggernaut turn it around?

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts JR Research, Investing Group Leader for Ultimate Growth Investing, and Dr. Christopher Davis of Quad 7 Capital for their thoughts on what lies ahead.

JR Research: UnitedHealth (UNH) stunned investors by changing its CEO abruptly, although the transition is assessed to be "less disruptive" as new CEO Stephen Hemsley has proved himself as he previously led UNH between 2006 and 2017 and is also its current chair. During his tenure as CEO, Hemsley also helped shape UnitedHealth (UNH) into the monolith that we know today. 

It remains to be seen, however, whether Hemsley's return can reinvigorate a remarkable turnaround amidst the current regulatory headwinds, pricing and utilization challenges emanating from its aggressive enrollment growth. With management maintaining its confidence of returning UNH to its long-term growth trajectory of between 13% to 16%, I believe the UNH story isn't dead yet, suggesting investors must focus on execution over the next two to three quarters to assess the plausible turnaround opportunity further. 

Quad 7 Capital: After this last kitchen sink flush, we highlighted that UNH was in chaos, but presented a potential generational entry opportunity into a world class, blue chip stock. 

Look, a surprise overnight CEO exit and uncertainty around WHY the company is experiencing high costs, on top of its Q1 report and guidance cut, deserved a selloff. But the stock is incredibly cheap historically. Bringing back a tremendous leader in Stephen Hemsley is bullish. He will right the ship. 

We are not saying this stock returns to $500-$600 in a few weeks. But we do think the stock finds a base at around $300. We suggested readers consider buying in $15 drops on the day of the flush. We think that while catching the bottom is improbable, it is possible; this is the type of selloff you have to take advantage of. 

We expect as early as the Q2 earnings we will get some sort of guidance. If not, then in Q3. We will get more clarity on the costs and the plan to address it. For traders with a medium-term horizon, look for a mean reversion trade back over $350-$360 later this year, provided the outlook does not change to project no growth in 2026.
